resolved_objections.resolution: RESOLVED. The broken first dispatcher implementation and the 'Actually, reconsider' narrative have been removed. Technical Hints now begins with 'Rejected approach (not used)' and presents only the correct push-first dispatcher structure. Verified: only one dispatcher implementation is present in the updated spec.

SUPERSEDED BY OBJ-7 (iteration 2), which itself is partially resolved and superseded by OBJ-8 (iteration 3). The compound-command security analysis in Edge Case 5 was added per OBJ-2's request; now complete and correct in iteration 4.
RESOLVED. M4 explicitly includes replacing _DO_IS_SUB with a reference to $IS_SUBAGENT. AC6 includes the criterion. Technical Hints M4 section includes exact replacement code. Verified.

RESOLVED. 'sole consume mechanism' is replaced throughout with 'sole PreToolUse allowlist match/approval call site in pretool-bash-safety.sh; actual grant deletion remains deferred to posttool-allowlist-consume.py (PostToolUse)'. Verified in both M3 requirement text and AC4 THEN clause.

RESOLVED. M3 now includes removal of the stale comment at lines 690-696. M4 now includes update of the stale comment at lines 124-131. The comment at lines 1064-1068 is addressed by noting it relocates with the IS_SUBAGENT variable assignment. Verified.

RESOLVED. The allow-6 analysis now correctly states posttool-allowlist-consume.py implements its own independent grant-read and consume logic at lines 38-104, does NOT use hooks/lib/allowlist.py, imports only lib.subagent for subagent detection, and is therefore a FOURTH independent implementation. Verified against actual file.

RESOLVED via OBJ-8 fix. BA's rebuttal was correct for the /allow grant path: full-string substring matching means a 'git push' pattern matches the full compound string, so the prior M2 return did not create a regression on that path. OBJ-8 (the real regression on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path) is now resolved by Option B.

RESOLVED by Option B. The prior M2 added 'return' after _evaluate_push in the dispatcher, which skipped _evaluate_commit for compound commands on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path. Option B eliminates this by: (1) adding a central fast-path conditioned on 'not _looks_like_git_push(command)' as the FIRST check, (2) retaining ALL 5 per-function _check_git_allowlist calls, and (3) NOT adding 'return' after the _evaluate_push dispatch. Verified via logic trace of three scenarios: (a) pure git commit with /allow grant — fast-path fires, ALLOWED correctly; (b) compound push+commit with CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1, push grant, no /allow, no commit grant — fast-path skipped (push present), push allowed via env, commit reaches per-function check, default-deny blocks — BLOCKED correctly; (c) force push with /allow grant — fast-path skipped (push present), absolute ban fires before allowlist check — BLOCKED correctly. AC8 encodes scenario (b) as a testable criterion.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex Finding 1+2 (PROPOSED_FIX, classified out_of_scope by QA): Option B moves push evaluation BEFORE forbidden_plumbing/reset_hard/direct_ref_mutation in the dispatcher. The current dispatcher runs forbidden_plumbing first. For a compound command containing BOTH a push subcommand AND a forbidden plumbing/reset/direct-ref-mutation subcommand: (a) under current code, forbidden_plumbing blocks immediately and the single-use push grant is preserved; (b) under Option B, _evaluate_push runs first and consumes the push grant (via _unlink_grant on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path at line 598), then forbidden_plumbing blocks. The compound command is blocked in both cases — no bypass is created. However, the push grant is consumed under Option B even though the overall command is blocked, which is a behavioral side-effect not acknowledged in the spec. Additionally, error-message diagnostic precedence changes for certain compound commands. QA classification: out_of_scope because (1) no bypass is opened or closed, (2) the spec's stated goal is 'keep the set of bypassable operations exactly identical to today's' and blocking-is-blocking satisfies this invariant, (3) the grant-consumption ordering issue arises only for degenerate compound commands that mix a push with a separately-forbidden operation, which are not realistic user workflows. Routed to BA for awareness in the next cycle if relevant.

obj8_fix_correct.rationale: Option B correctly eliminates the OBJ-8 regression. The central fast-path fires ONLY when _looks_like_git_push is False, ensuring the fast-path can never short-circuit push evaluation. No 'return' is added after _evaluate_push, so all downstream dispatches (commit, merge, etc.) still run. Per-function _check_git_allowlist calls are RETAINED in all 5 non-push evaluate-* functions, preserving the safety-net checks for compound commands where CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE allows push but no broader /allow grant exists.

obj1_through_obj7_still_resolved.rationale: No changes to M1, M3, M4 in iteration 4. Those requirements and their ACs are unchanged from iteration 3. Prior resolutions of OBJ-1 through OBJ-6 remain intact. OBJ-7 is now fully resolved via the OBJ-8 fix.

ac2_correctness.rationale: AC2 now requires: (1) central call present conditioned on 'not _looks_like_git_push(command)' as FIRST substantive check — correctly captures Option B fast-path structure; (2) all per-function calls in 5 non-push evaluate-* functions RETAINED explicitly; (3) _evaluate_push retains own call AFTER _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation explicitly stated; (4) no 'return' added inside _looks_like_git_push branch explicitly stated; (5) comment explaining why push is excluded required. All five clauses are correct and independently verifiable.

ac8_correctness.rationale: AC8 directly encodes the OBJ-8 scenario: GIVEN CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 + valid push grant, no /allow grant, no commit grant; WHEN command is 'git push origin main; git commit -m x'; THEN hook exits 2 with 'BLOCKED: agent git commit'. Logic trace confirms this AC is satisfiable by the Option B dispatcher: fast-path skipped (push detected), _evaluate_push allows push via env/grant, falls through to _evaluate_commit, per-function check returns False (no /allow grant), commit grant absent, _block_default_deny_commit fires.

no_new_objections.rationale: Option B only ADDS a fast-path check and does NOT remove any existing checks. Codex confirmed (Finding 3, OBSERVATION_ONLY) that the central fast-path does not create a new allowlist bypass for non-push commands — the same full-string matching logic means the fast-path allows exactly what per-function calls would have allowed. The dispatcher reordering (push before plumbing) is a behavioral side-effect noted as out_of_scope_observations but does not constitute a scope violation per the stated requirements (no bypass opened, no bypass closed). Scope invariants verified: force push remains blocked (AC3 logic trace passes), systemctl bypass via global short-circuit still works (AC5 — M3 does not touch line 615), subagent firewall intact (AC7 — M4 does not change the IS_SUBAGENT=1 return logic).

scope_invariant_check.rationale: M2 Option B only ADDS the central fast-path — it does not remove any currently-active check. The 5 per-function calls are explicitly retained. The push absolute-ban (_push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation) is not touched. The CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path is not touched. No new bypass surface is opened: 'not _looks_like_git_push' condition ensures push commands never hit the fast-path return, preserving all push-related guards. No currently-bypassable surface is closed: the fast-path with _check_git_allowlist has identical allow-logic to the per-function calls it optimizes.

scenario_b_compound_push_commit_claude_p.command: git push origin main; git commit -m 'x'
scenario_b_compound_push_commit_claude_p.context: CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1, push grant, no /allow grant, no commit grant
scenario_b_compound_push_commit_claude_p.evaluate_commit_result: _check_git_allowlist returns False (no /allow), commit grant absent, _block_default_deny_commit fires

qa.summary: Iteration 4 review: all 8 prior objections resolved. Option B correctly eliminates OBJ-8 by adding a central fast-path conditioned on 'not _looks_like_git_push(command)', retaining all 5 per-function _check_git_allowlist calls, and omitting 'return' after push dispatch. AC2 is correctly respecified with all five required properties. AC8 is a correct and testable new criterion encoding the OBJ-8 scenario. Codex adversarial review found no security regression or bypass: Finding 1+2 (push-first reordering causes push-grant to be consumed before forbidden-plumbing blocks in degenerate compound commands) are classified out_of_scope — no bypass is opened or closed, the spec's invariant is satisfied. Finding 3 confirmed the fast-path creates no new bypass. Verdict: PASS.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Finding 1 (PROPOSED_FIX): Push-first reordering is a behavioral change due to push-grant side effects — _evaluate_push unlinks the push grant on CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path (line 598), so for compound push+forbidden-plumbing, Option B consumes the grant before blocking while current code preserves the grant. Not a new bypass, but grant-consumption semantics differ. Proposed fix: keep original dispatch order (forbidden_plumbing -> reset_hard -> direct_ref_mutation -> push) after central fast-path. Finding 2 (PROPOSED_FIX): Diagnostic precedence changes for compound commands (which block message fires first). Same root cause as Finding 1. Finding 3 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): Central fast-path does not create new allowlist bypass — same full-string matching logic means fast-path allows exactly what per-function calls would allow. Finding 4: OBJ-8 fix is correct given no return after push and per-function calls retained.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1+2 evaluated and classified as out_of_scope_observations. The grant-consumption side-effect for degenerate compound push+forbidden-plumbing commands is not a bypass. The spec's invariant is 'keep bypassable operations exactly identical to today's' — blocking-is-blocking satisfies this. Routing push-before-plumbing ordering concern to out_of_scope_observations for BA awareness in a future cycle. Finding 3 (OBSERVATION_ONLY) confirmed QA's fast-path bypass-neutrality analysis. No blocking issues from Codex. Verdict remains PASS.
